video_title: The Sandman: Season 2 | Volume 2 Official Teaser | Netflix
reply_text: One thing I love is how the Endless speak with an ancient, cosmic presence, each word measured, deliberate, and heavy with meaning. Delirium is the exception, her speech is almost childlike, fragmented, and unpredictably whimsical, reflecting her chaotic nature. The others especially Dream sound as though they have traversed the universe for billions of years and of course they have. Lucifer once mentioned having been alongside Dream 10 billion years ago and wonder what he was like back then. No word is ever rushed; every syllable is carefully weighed and controlled, and the actors are able to portrayed that nicely even  the actor who plays Delirium since that is her character and she speaks that way. This is how imagine immortals would speak to each other. Michael in Supernatural after he took over Winchesters younger brother replied "no-one is home right now" or something like that when Dean called his brother's name and a remember reading a comment stating "doesn't sound like someone who being around for millions of years" lol.
